scenes.narration: Narcissists. Psychopaths. Sociopaths. The internet calls them toxic. But what does their brain actually want?
scenes.image_prompt: A person's face reflected in a polished mirror, reflection slightly distorted and unsettling compared to the real face, dramatic lighting with shadows, black and white photorealistic, unsettling symmetry, vertical composition

scenes.narration: The narcissist wants one thing above all else. Admiration. They need you to believe they're exceptional. Smarter. Better. More important than you.
scenes.image_prompt: A man standing on a pedestal alone in darkness, harsh spotlight creating theatrical effect, surrounding shadows with silhouettes of people below looking up in admiration, black and white photorealistic, hierarchical power composition

scenes.narration: The psychopath wants control. Not power for power's sake. Control over outcomes. They see the world as a game where they keep score and always win.
scenes.image_prompt: A close-up of hands moving chess pieces on a board, one piece being moved with deliberate precision, fallen pieces scattered below, dark board, dramatic shadows, black and white photorealistic metallic texture

scenes.narration: The sociopath is different. They want what feels good right now. No plan, no narcissism. Just immediate gratification and no internal punishment for breaking your rules.
scenes.image_prompt: A person in dynamic motion caught mid-action, blurred movement suggesting chaos and impulse, broken objects scattered around them, impulsive energy frozen in time, black and white photorealistic, dynamic unsettling composition

scenes.narration: Research shows they're not monsters. They're not broken. They're optimized for a different goal. They feel what you feel. They just don't care that you feel it.
scenes.image_prompt: extreme close-up of a person's eyes very close to camera, pupils dilated, watching intently with no warmth or connection visible, technical clinical observation, black and white photorealistic intense stare, psychological discomfort, vertical frame

scenes.narration: Here's what's disturbing. You already know a few. Maybe multiple. You passed them in the street. Worked for them. Trusted them.
scenes.image_prompt: Silhouettes of people in a crowded street or public space, one figure turned slightly toward camera, subtle menace in their posture and gaze, everyday urban setting made eerie, black and white photorealistic uncanny, vertical composition

scenes.narration: They're not evil. They're not tortured. They simply live without the rules your conscience writes every single day. And that works for them.
scenes.image_prompt: A person walking alone on a path or street, shadows around them, calm composed expression showing no remorse or internal conflict, light and shadow perfectly balanced, black and white photorealistic, tranquility mixed with subtle menace
